figures.caption: Figure 1. Overview of Chart-RL Framework. We !rst use data pipeline to construct training samples from pre-processed CQA dataset, then factorize policy optimization based reinforcement learning (GRPO/DAPO/GSPO) in the training pipeline. The trained policy model is then used in the inference pipeline to complete question-answering task through the enhanced visual understanding and reasoning by the VLMs.

Figure 2. Reinforcement Learning Processes by GRPO, DAPO and GSPO. The training loss represents model’s error decreasing as it learns from the training data. The RL reward indicates the upward trend over time during the successful training run. The policy entropy demonstrates a stable non-zero level indicating the active, $exible and exploring training process. The episode mean length shows rapid and stable convergence over the reinforcement learning.
Figure 3. Latency-Accuracy trade-o# analysis. Our RLenhanced models achieve an optimal balance between qualitative performance and computational e"ciency.

Table 1. Model Performance Comparison. The !rst set of models are the state-of-the-art (SOTA) proprietary (or closedsource) MLLMs, the second set are the open-source models, while the third set are our !ne-tuned Qwen models
